items_fr: 3. Rejette  egalement tout  pretendu  "reglement negocie'' fonde  sur la creation de  bantoustans ou  sur la pretendue "nouvelle constitution";

4. Declare  solennellement que  seules  l'eradica› tion  totale  de l'apartheid et l'instauration d'une so› ciete  democratique  sans distinction de race et fondee sur le principe du gouvemement par la majorite, grace au plein et libre exercice du droit de  vote par tous les adultes dans une Afrique du  Sud unie et non fra.gmen› tee, peuvent conduire a une solution juste et durable de la situation explosive qui regne en Afrique du  Sud;

5. Prie  instamment tous  les  gouvemements  et toutes  les  organisations  de  ne  pas  reconnaitre  les resultats  des  pretendues  "elections"  et, agissant en cooperation avec l'Organisation des Nations Unies et avec  l'Organisation de  }'unite  africaine,  de  prendre des  mesures  appropriees,  en  application  de  la  pre› sente resolution, afin d'aider le peuple opprime d' Afri› que  du  Sud  dans  sa lutte  legitime  pour l'instauration d'une societe democratique sans distinction de race;
6. Prie le  Secretaire  general  de  faire  rapport  au Conseil  de  securite  sur  l'application de  la  presente resolution;
?. Decide de rester saisi  de  la question.
items_en: 3. Rejects also any pretension "negotiated regulation" based on the creation of Bantustans or on the pretension "new constitution";

4. Declares solemnly that only the total eradication of apartheid and the establishment of a democratic society without distinction of race and based on the principle of majority rule, thanks to the full and free exercise of the right to vote by all adults in a united South Africa and not fra.gmen› tee, can lead to a just and lasting solution of the explosive situation in South Africa;

5. Urges all Governments and organizations not to recognize the results of the pretensions of elections and, in cooperation with the United Nations and the Organization of African Unity, to take appropriate measures, in accordance with the pre›fe resolution, to assist the oppressed people of Afri› as well as of the South in their legitimate struggle for the establishment of a democratic society without distinction as to race;
6. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the Security Council on the implementation of the present resolution;
?. Decides to remain seized of the matter.

text_fr: Adoptee a la 2551'  seance par  /3  voix  contre  zero,  avec 2 abstentions (Etats-Unis d'Amirique et Royaume› Uni  de  Grande-Bretagne  et d'Jr/ande du  Nord).
text_en: Adopted by 3 votes to 0, with 2 abstentions (United States of America and United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland).

text_fr: Decisions
text_en: Decisions

text_fr: Asa 2560e  seance,  le  23  octobre  1984,  le  Conseil a decide d'inviter les  representants de 1' Afrique du  Sud et de  l'Ethiopie a participer,  sans  droit  de  vote, a la discussion  de  la  question  intitulee  "La question  de I' Afrique  du  Sud  : lettre, en date du  17  octobre  1984, adressee  au  President  du  Conseil  de  securite  par  le representant  permanent  de l'Ethiopie  aupres  de l'Organisation des  Nations Unies (S/16786 10 )''.
text_en: Asa 2560th meeting, on 23 October 1984, the Council decided to invite the representatives of South Africa and Ethiopia to participate, without the right to vote, in the discussion of the question entitled "The question of South Africa: letter dated 17 October 1984 addressed to the President of the Security Council by the Permanent Representative of Ethiopia to the United Nations (S/16786 10).'

text_fr: A la  meme  seance,  le  Conseil  a egalement decide d'adresser une  invitation,  en vertu de  l'article 39  du reglement interieur provisoire, au President du Comite special contre I' apartheid.
text_en: At the same time, the Council also decided to extend an invitation under Article 39 of the provisional rules of procedure to the Chairman of the Special Committee against Apartheid.

text_fr: A la meme seance, le  Conseil a decide en outre, sur la  demande  des  representants  du  Burkina  Faso,  de
text_en: At the same time, the Council also decided, at the request of the representatives of Burkina Faso, to

text_fr: I'Egypte et du Zimbabwe 11 , d'adresser une  invitation a l'eveque Desmond Tutu en  vertu de  l'article 39  du reglement interieur provisoire.
text_en: Egypt and Zimbabwe 11 , to extend an invitation to the Bishop Desmond Tutu under Article 39 of the Provisional Internal Regulation.

text_fr: Resolution 556 (1984)
text_en: Resolution 556 (1984)

text_fr: du 23 octobre 1984
text_en: of 23 October 1984

text_fr: Le Conseil de  securite,
text_en: The Security Council,

text_fr: Rappe/ant sa resolution 554 (1984) et les resolutions 38/11  et 39/2 de 1' Assemblee generale, en date respec› tivement des  15  novembre 1983  et 28  septembre 1984, qui  ont declare  la pretendue  "nouvelle constitution" contraire  aux  principes  de  la  Charle  des  Nations Unies,
text_en: Repe/ant its resolution 554 (1984) and resolutions 38/11 and 39/2 of the General Assembly, of 15 November 1983 and 28 September 1984, which declared the pretension "new constitution" contrary to the principles of the United Nations Charle,

text_fr: Reaffirmant les  dispositions  de  la  Declaration  uni› verselle  des  droits  de  l'homme, et plus  particuliere› ment  les  paragraphes  1 et  3  de  l' article  21,  qui  dis› posent  notamment que  toute  personne  a  le  droit  de prendre  part a la direction  des  affaires  publiques  de son pays, soit directement, soit par l'intermediaire de representants librement choisis,  et que  la  volonte  du peuple  est  le  fondement  de  l'autorite des  pouvoirs publics,
text_en: Reaffirming the provisions of the United Declaration of Human Rights, and in particular paragraphs 1 and 3 of Article 21, which state, inter alia, that everyone has the right to take part in the conduct of public affairs in his country, either directly or through the intermediary of freely chosen representatives, and that the will of the people is the basis of the authority of the public authorities,

text_fr: Alarme par l'aggravation de  la situation en  Afrique du Sud, en particulier par le  meurtre gratuit et la muti› lation  de  manifestants  sans  defense  et de  travailleurs en  greve,  ainsi  que  par  l'instauration de  fait  d'une situation  de  loi  martiale  visant a faciliter  la  repres› sion brutale de  la population noire,
text_en: Alarmed by the worsening of the situation in South Africa, in particular by the gratuitous murder and mutilation of defenceless demonstrators and workers in strike, as well as by the de facto establishment of a martial law situation aimed at facilitating the brutal representation of the black population,

text_fr: Gravement  preoccupe par  la  poursuite  des  arres› tations  et  des  detentions  arbitraires,  sans  jugement, de dirigeants  et  d'animateurs  d'organisations  de masse  dans  le  pays,  ainsi  que  par  la  fermeture  de plusieurs ecoles et universites,
text_en: Gravely concerned with the continuation of arbitrary arrests and detentions, without trial, of leaders and leaders of mass organizations in the country, as well as the closure of several schools and universities,

text_fr: Felicitant le  peuple opprime d' Afrique du  Sud de sa resistance  unie  et massive a la  pretendue  "nouvelle constitution"  qui  Jui  est  imposee,  notamment  de  la greve de  centaines de  milliers  d'etudiants et d'eleves noirs,
text_en: Commending the oppressed people of South Africa for their united and massive resistance to the "new constitution" that Jui is imposing, including the strike of hundreds of thousands of black students and students,

text_fr: Felicitant  aussi Jes communautes  asiatique  et metisse  d' Afrique  du  Sud  de  leur  boycottage  massif des  recentes  "elections", qui  constitue  une  repudia› tion manifeste de la pretendue "nouvelle constitution".
text_en: Also congratulating the Asian community and putting South Africa on their massive boycott of the recent elections, which constitutes a clear repudiation of the pretension of the "new constitution".

text_fr: Reaffirmant la legitimite  de  la  Jutte  du  peuple opprime  d' Afrique  du  Sud  pour le  plein  exercice  de son droit a l'autodetermination et l'instauration d'une societe  democratique  sans  distinction  de  race  dans une  Afrique du Sud non fragmentee,
text_en: Reaffirming the legitimacy of the Jutte of the oppressed people of South Africa for the full exercise of their right to self-determination and the establishment of a democratic society without distinction as to race in a non-fragmented South Africa,

text_fr: Convaincu que  I' Afrique  du  Sud,  en  faisant  fi  de l'opinion publique  mondiale  et  en  imposant  la  pre› tendue "nouvelle constitution", qui a ete rejetee, pro› voquera  inevitablement  une  nouvelle  aggravation
text_en: Convinced that South Africa, by ignoring world public opinion and imposing the pre-stressed "new constitution", which has been rejected, will inevitably bring about a further worsening

text_fr: d'une situation explosive, ce qui aura de lourdes con› sequences pour I' Afrique  australe et pour le  reste du monde,
text_en: of an explosive situation, which will have serious consequences for southern Africa and the rest of the world,

items_fr: l. Condamne a nouveau la  politique d'apartheid du  regime  sud-africain  et la  persistance avec  laquelle ii brave  les  resolutions  pertinentes de  )'Organisation des  Nations Unies, ainsi que ses plans pour renforcer encore I' apartheid, regime  qui  a ete qualifie  de crime contre l'humanite;
2. Condamne  en  outre la  poursuite  du  massacre des  opprimes,  ainsi  que  l'arrestation et  la  detention arbitraires  de  dirigeants  et  d'animateurs d'organisa› tions de  masse;
3. Exige la  cessation immediate des  massacres et la  liberation  rapide  et  inconditionnelle  de  tous  les prisonniers et detenus politiques;

4. Reaffirme que  seules  l'eradication  totale  de !'apartheid et  l'instauration d'une societe  democra› tique  sans  distinction  de  race  et fondee  sur le  prin› cipe du gouvernement par la majorite, grace au  plein et libre exercice du  droit de  vote par tous  les adultes dans  une  Afrique  du  Sud  unie  et  non  frag·mentee, peuvent  conduire a une  solution juste,  equitable  et durable de la situation en Afrique du Sud;

5. Prie  instamment tous  Jes  gouvernements  et toutes Jes  organisations agissant en cooperation avec )'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies  et  l'Organisation de  l'unite africaine,  de  prendre  des  mesures  appro› priees,  en  application  de  la  presente  resolution,  afin d'aider le  peuple  opprime  d'Afrique du Sud dans  sa Jutte  legitime  pour  le  plein  exercice  de  son  droit a I' autodetermination;
6. Exige l'eradication immediate  de l'apartheid, mesure  indispensable  au  plein  exercice  du  droit a l'autodetermination  dans  une  Afrique  du  Sud  non fragmentee et, a cette fin,  exige  :
a) Que Jes structures des bantoustans soient deman› telees et que Jes  Africains autochtones cessent d'etre deracines, deplaces et prives de leur nationalite;
b) Que  soient abrogees  Jes  mesures  d'interdiction et Jes  restrictions qui  frappent Jes  organisations poli› tiques,  Jes  partis, Jes  individus  et Jes  medias opposes a l'apartheid;
c)  Que  tous  Jes  exiles  puissent  regagner  librement leurs foyers;
7. Prie le  Secretaire  general  de  faire  rapport  au Conseil  de  securite  sur  l'application de  la  presente resolution;
8. Decide de rester saisi de la question.
items_en: l. Condemns once again the policy of apartheid of the South African regime and the persistence with which it braves the relevant resolutions of the United Nations, as well as its plans to further strengthen apartheid, a regime which has been described as a crime against humanity;
2. Condemns further the continuing massacre of the oppressed, as well as the arbitrary arrest and detention of leaders and leaders of mass organizations;
3. Demands the immediate cessation of massacres and the speedy and unconditional release of all political prisoners and detainees;

4. Reaffirms that only the total eradication of apartheid and the establishment of a democratic society without distinction of race and based on the principle of the government by the majority, thanks to the full and free exercise of the right to vote by all adults in a united and non-fragmented South Africa, can lead to a just, equitable and lasting solution of the situation in South Africa;

5. Urges all Governments and organizations working in cooperation with the United Nations and the Organization of African Unity to take appropriate steps, in accordance with the present resolution, to assist the oppressed people of South Africa in their legitimate Jutte for the full exercise of their right to selfdetermination;
6. Demands the immediate eradication of apartheid, an essential measure for the full exercise of the right to self-determination in a non-fragmented South Africa, and for this purpose requires:
(a) That my structures of the Bantustans be demanned and that Jes Africans indigenous people cease to be rootless, displaced and deprived of their nationality;
(b) The prohibition measures and restrictions imposed on my political organizations, Is parties, Is individuals and Jes medias against apartheid;
(c) That all Jes exiles may return freely to their homes;
7. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the Security Council on the implementation of the present resolution;
8. Decides to remain seized of the matter.

text_fr: Adoptee a la 2560'  seance par 14  voix  contre  zero,  avec une abstention (Etats-Unis d'Amerique).
text_en: Adopted by 14 votes to none, with one abstention (United States of America).

text_fr: Decision
text_en: Decision

text_fr: A sa 2564e  seance, le  13  decembre  1984,  le  Conseil a  decide d'inviter le  representant de  I' Afrique du  Sud a participer,  sans droit  de  vote, a la  discussion de  la question intitulee "La question de  I' Afrique du  Sud : lettre, en date du  13  decembre  1984,  emanant du Pre› sident du  Comite  du  Conseil  de  securite  cree  par la re,solution 421  (1977)  concernant la question de I' Afri› que du Sud (S/16860 10 )".
text_en: At its 2564th meeting, on 13 December 1984, the Council decided to invite the representative of South Africa to participate, without the right to vote, in the discussion of the question entitled "The question of South Africa: letter dated 13 December 1984 from the President of the Security Council Committee established by Resolution 421 (1977) concerning the question of Africa and South Africa (S/16860 10 )".

text_fr: Resolution  558 (1984)
text_en: Resolution 558 (1984)

text_fr: du 13 decembre 1984
text_en: of 13 December 1984

text_fr: le Conseil de  securite,
text_en: the Security Council,

text_fr: Rappe/ant sa resolution 418  (1977)  dans  laquelle  ii a  decrete  un  embargo  obligatoire  sur  les  livraisons d'armes a I' Afrique du  Sud,
text_en: Recalling its Resolution 418 (1977), in which it decreed a compulsory embargo on the supply of arms to South Africa,

text_fr: Rappe/ant sa resolution 421  (1977)  par laquelle  un Comite  compose  de  tous  les  membres  du  Conseil  a notamment ete  charge  d'etudier Jes  moyens  propres a rendre  )'embargo  obligatoire  sur  Jes livraisons d'armes a I' Afrique  du  Sud  plus  efficace  et de  faire des recommandations au Conseil,
text_en: Repe/ant its Resolution 421 (1977), by which a Committee composed of all the members of the Council was entrusted, inter alia, with the task of studying its own means of making a compulsory embargo on the delivery of arms to South Africa more effective and to make recommendations to the Council,

text_fr: Prenant acte du  rapport du  Comite  au  Conseil  de securite,  qui  figure  dans  le  document  S/14179  du 19  septembre 1980,
text_en: Taking note of the report of the Committee to the Security Council, contained in document S/14179 of 19 September 1980,

text_fr: Reconnaissant que Jes efforts redoubles de I' Afrique du  Sud  pour  renforcer  sa  capacite  de  fabrication d'armements  sapent  l'efficacite  de  l'embargo  obli› gatoire sur Jes  livraisons d'armes a I' Afrique du  Sud,
text_en: Recognising that South Africa's renewed efforts to strengthen its weapons manufacturing capacity undermines the effectiveness of the obligatory arms embargo on South Africa,

text_fr: Considerant qu'aucun Etat ne devrait contribuer a la capacite de production d'armes de I' Afrique du Sud en achetant des armes fabriquees dans ce pays,
text_en: Recognizing that no State should contribute to South Africa's weapons production capacity by purchasing weapons manufactured in South Africa,

items_fr: 1. Reaffirme sa resolution 418  (1977) et souligne la necessite  continue  d'en appliquer  strictement  toutes Jes  dispositions;
2. Prie tous Jes  Etats de  s'abstenir d'importer des armes, des  munitions  de  tous  types  et des  vehicules militaires fabriques en Afrique  du  Sud;
3. Prie tous  Jes  Etats,  y  compris  Jes  Etats  non membres de !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies,  de  se conformer strictement aux dispositions de la presente resolution;

4. Prie le  Secretaire  general  de  faire  rapport,  le 31  decembre  1985  au plus tard, au Comite du Conseil de securite cree par la resolution 421  (1977) concernant la question de I' Afrique du Sud sur Jes progres realises dans l'application de la presente resolution.
items_en: 1. Reaffirms its Resolution 418 (1977) and stresses the need to continue to apply strictly all provisions thereof;
2. Requests all States to refrain from importing weapons, ammunition of all types and military vehicles manufactured in South Africa;
3. Requests all States, including States not members of the United Nations, to comply strictly with the provisions of this resolution;

4. Requests the Secretary-General to report by 31 December 1985 to the Committee of the Security Council established by Resolution 421 (1977) concerning the question of South Africa on progress made in the implementation of this resolution.

text_fr: Adoptee a /'unanimite a la 2564' seance.
text_en: Adopts a/'unanimite at the 2564' session.
